Republican continuity via elections; hereditary titles banned but propertied class continuity assumed.

Amendment process and checks/balances designed for long-term institutional durability.

Designed to survive Confederation collapse and external threats via stronger central powers.

Federal authority over defense, commerce, and insurrections; boundaries consolidated but contested.
Checks/balances, amendment process, and separation of powers as error-correction mechanisms.

Written Constitution, Federalist Papers, ratification records as stable institutional memory.
Broad Anglo-Protestant baseline but significant regional/sectarian variation tolerated.
